response: The internet originated from US Department of Defense research in the 1960s, with ARPANET's first message sent in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 forming the modern internet's foundation. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN in 1989, and the Mosaic browser (1993) brought it to mainstream users, fueling the dot-com boom and producing giants like Amazon and Google. Subsequent developments—social media in the mid-2000s, mobile internet after the iPhone's 2007 launch, and cloud computing led by AWS (2006)—made the internet ubiquitous. Today, it connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly all aspects of modern life.
